title: hieracium vulgatum subsp. ramosifolium
intro: Hieracium vulgatum subsp. ramosifolium, also known as the hawkweed plant, is a subspecies of the Hieracium vulgatum species, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. The hawkweed plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is said to have several health benefits, including the ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and relieve digestive issues. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the hawkweed plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
